---
title: "Day 48 — HELM, MedHELM, and the distinctions a single score erases"
subtitle: "Wednesday, 9 September 2026 · Week 7 · Pipeline, RAG, evaluation · Study day · 3-4 h"
---

::: {.day-nav .kind-study}
[← Day 47](day-47.qmd) · [All 50 days](../timeline.qmd) · [Day 49 →](day-49.qmd)
:::

::: {.why}
For clinical work the evaluation is the deliverable, and HELM's contribution was structural rather than numerical: it insists that every model be scored on every scenario across several metrics at once, which makes visible the trade-offs a leaderboard column hides.
:::

## What you should be able to do tonight

- You can name the metric categories HELM measures for every scenario and say which of them your Day 35 report already covers.
- You can point at two MedHELM scenarios and say precisely what a high score there does and does not predict about a clinician's actual workflow.
- You can classify a given failure as a model failure, a retrieval failure, an interface failure or a workflow failure, and name the evidence that settles it.

## Tasks <span class='task-total'>205 min</span>

::: {.task-list}
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> [55 min]{.task-min} Read the HELM paper (arXiv 2211.09110) Sections 1-3: the scenario-by-metric taxonomy, the argument for dense coverage over cherry-picked benchmarks, and the seven metric categories measured for each scenario - accuracy, calibration, robustness, fairness, bias, toxicity, efficiency. Write down which of the seven you have ever measured on your own task.
:::
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> [35 min]{.task-min} Open HELM Capabilities and HELM Lite. Pick one model and read across its metrics instead of down the ranking, then find a pair of models whose order flips depending on which metric you sort by. Record the pair and the two metrics.
:::
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> [50 min]{.task-min} Work through MedHELM. Count how many of its scenarios are built from real clinical records or clinician-authored workflows versus how many are exam-style multiple choice, then pick the two scenarios closest to your PubMed abstract task and write down what each actually asks the model to do.
:::
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> [45 min]{.task-min} Write eval_principles.md: six distinctions - capability vs reliability, average vs worst case, knowledge vs reasoning, correctness vs persuasiveness, benchmark score vs real-world utility, and model failure vs retrieval/interface/workflow failure. For each, one line on which side your current evaluation measures, and one line on what you would have to build to measure the other.
:::
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> [20 min]{.task-min} Record what maintenance mode since 2026-06-01 means for using HELM going forward: which parts stay useful indefinitely (the taxonomy, the scenario definitions, the released code) and which decay (leaderboard coverage of anything released after that date).
:::
:::

## Resources

- [Holistic Evaluation of Language Models (HELM)](https://arxiv.org/abs/2211.09110) [paper]{.res-kind}
- [MedHELM leaderboard](https://crfm.stanford.edu/helm/medhelm/latest/) [leaderboard]{.res-kind}
- [HELM Capabilities](https://crfm.stanford.edu/helm/capabilities/latest/) [leaderboard]{.res-kind}
- [HELM Lite](https://crfm.stanford.edu/helm/lite/latest/) [leaderboard]{.res-kind}
- [HELM docs](https://crfm-helm.readthedocs.io/en/latest/) [docs]{.res-kind}
- [HELM source](https://github.com/stanford-crfm/helm) [repo]{.res-kind}

## Deliverable

::: {.deliverable}
eval_principles.md - six distinctions, each with the side your evaluation currently measures and the concrete thing you would build to measure the other - plus the model pair whose ranking flips between two metrics.
:::

## Checkpoint

::: {.checkpoint}
A model scores 0.89 on a MedHELM scenario named after a clinical workflow - what are two reasons that number can be true while the model is still unsafe to deploy in that workflow?
:::

## Prepare for tomorrow

Ten minutes tonight so Day 49, Thursday, 10 September 2026 starts clean. This is what the evening email sends you.

::: {.task-list .prep}
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> Name the two or three models you will evaluate tomorrow and confirm each one loads.
:::
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> Locate your held-out split and verify from your logs that no prompt, threshold or checkpoint was ever selected using it.
:::
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> Reread your Day 35 failure taxonomy so tomorrow's version extends it instead of restarting it.
:::
:::
